pattern: By inclusion-exclusion the covered area is the sum of the two rectangle areas minus their overlap, where the overlap is the product of the positive horizontal and vertical interval intersections (zero when they do not overlap).
hints: Adding the two areas double-counts any region they share, so subtract that shared region once.
The overlap is itself an axis-aligned rectangle: its width is the intersection of the two x-intervals and its height the intersection of the two y-intervals.
Width = max(0, min(ax2,bx2) - max(ax1,bx1)) and height likewise on y; covered = areaA + areaB - width*height.

edge_inputs.description: identical rectangles overlap fully, area not doubled
touching edge has zero overlap width
one rectangle fully inside the other

stress.description: random axis-aligned rectangle pairs across the coordinate range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    def rect():
        x1 = rng.randint(-10000, 10000)
        x2 = rng.randint(x1, 10000)
        y1 = rng.randint(-10000, 10000)
        y2 = rng.randint(y1, 10000)
        return [x1, y1, x2, y2]
    a = rect()
    b = rect()
    return a + b
stress.note: constructs valid rectangles (x1<=x2, y1<=y2); a single argument set per seed since the function is O(1)

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def computeArea(self, ax1, ay1, ax2, ay2, bx1, by1, bx2, by2):
        areaA = (ax2 - ax1) * (ay2 - ay1)
        areaB = (bx2 - bx1) * (by2 - by1)
        # overlap via explicit interval intersection
        ox = min(ax2, bx2) - max(ax1, bx1)
        oy = min(ay2, by2) - max(ay1, by1)
        overlap = ox * oy if ox > 0 and oy > 0 else 0
        return areaA + areaB - overlap

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def computeArea(self, ax1, ay1, ax2, ay2, bx1, by1, bx2, by2):
        areaA = (ax2 - ax1) * (ay2 - ay1)
        areaB = (bx2 - bx1) * (by2 - by1)
        w = max(0, min(ax2, bx2) - max(ax1, bx1))
        h = max(0, min(ay2, by2) - max(ay1, by1))
        return areaA + areaB - w * h
